page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-64 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing hyperthreading with two threads per core. The instance is configured with 240 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing, evaluated via stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests, consistently falls into the lower tier. However, multi-core execution scales to average performance levels, as demonstrated by multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations also align with average tier expectations.

This hardware profile is suited for workloads that benefit from high core counts but do not require cutting-edge single-thread performance. Appropriate use cases include multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the balanced resource allocation of the N1 standard tier provides a predictable, mid-range performance profile for legacy workloads and general-purpose applications that do not justify the premium of newer architecture generations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-64 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs and 240 GB of RAM. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture, it allocates 32 physical cores with hyperthreading. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This makes it suitable for parallelized workloads, static web serving, and database operations that leverage high core counts without requiring high single-thread speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-64 is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs and 240 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual machine featuring sixty-four virtual CPUs and legacy Intel Xeon architecture for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 240 GB RAM powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Dedicated x86_64 CPU resources with 32 physical cores and hyperthreading
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth across standard benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and database operations
